title: lithocarpus leptogyne
intro: Lithocarpus leptogyne, also known as the 'Tanoak', is a species of evergreen tree native to the Pacific coast of North America, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The bark of the Lithocarpus leptogyne plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating gastrointestinal issues. Lithocarpus leptogyne is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a herbal remedy, and its extracts have also been used in traditional folk medicine.
